zh.lifeSummary: Francis Nicholas Marshall是Nicolas Marchal和Maria Palmontier的兒子，於1728年8月30日出生在法國巴-爾洛索奧。他於1749年4月29日與Marguerite Grandadam在法國巴-爾洛索奧結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1802年9月25日在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯享年74歲，埋葬在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯康內瓦格 查阿培爾 巴西利卡 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Italian Francesco , originally a vocabulary word meaning ‘French’ or ‘Frenchman’ (Late Latin Franciscus; compare Frank ). This was a nickname given to St Francis of Assisi ( 1181–1226 ) because of his wealthy father's business connections with France. His baptismal name was Giovanni . He had a pleasant, ordinary life as a child and young man, but after two serious illnesses, a period of military service, and a year as a prisoner of war in Perugia, he turned from the world and devoted himself to caring for the poor and sick. He was joined by groups of disciples, calling themselves ‘minor friars’ (friari minores). The main features of the Franciscan rule are humility, poverty, and love for all living creatures. The given name occurs occasionally in England as early as 1300 , and more frequently from the early 16th century, when there was a surge of admiration for, and imitation of, Italian Renaissance culture.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

historicEvents.description: France declares war on Austria over the question of Polish succession.
The Battle of Banja Luka. The Turkish army beats the Austrian army.
Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart is born in Salzburg, Austria.
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.

historicEvents.title: Second State to Ratify U.S. Constitution
historicEvents.description: On December 12, 1787, Pennsylvania ratified the U.S. Constitution.

en.lifeSummary: Francis Nicholas Marshall was born on 30 August 1728, in Rothau, Bas-Rhin, France as the son of Nicolas Marchal and Maria Palmontier. He married Marguerite Grandadam on 29 April 1749, in Rothau, Bas-Rhin, France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died on 25 September 1802, in Adams, Pennsylvania, United States, at the age of 74, and was buried in Conewago Chapel Basilica Cemetery, Adams, Pennsylvania, United States.

historicEvents.description: Les limites du Bas-Rhin furent modifiées à de nombreuses reprises : en 1793, il absorba les territoires suivants, nouvellement annexés par la France : le comté de Sarrewerden (Bouquenom et Sarrewerden étaient rattachés à la Moselle de 1790 à 1793), le comté de Drulingen, la seigneurie de Diemeringen, la seigneurie d'Asswiller, domaine de la famille de Steinkallenfels, plusieurs communes du Palatinat; en 1795 la région de Schirmeck - qui ne parlait pas l'alsacien - lui fut retirée (district de Sélestat) et rattachée aux Vosges (district de Senones) ; en 1808, des territoires à l'est du Rhin lui furent rattachés, en particulier la ville de Kehl ; en 1814, à la suite du premier traité de Paris, il gagna des territoires au nord de la Lauter, provenant de l'ancien département du Mont-Tonnerre, notamment la ville de Landau, mais il perdit tous les territoires à l'est du Rhin;

fr.lifeSummary: Francis Nicholas Marshall est né le 30 août 1728, à Rothau, Bas-Rhin, France, fils de Nicolas Marchal et Maria Palmontier. Il a épousé Marguerite Grandadam le 29 avril 1749, à Rothau, Bas-Rhin, France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé le 25 septembre 1802, à Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Conewago Chapel Basilica Cemetery, Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis.

ko.lifeSummary: Francis Nicholas Marshall(은)는 1728년 8월 30일 프랑스 바스-히 로스오에서 Nicolas Marchal(와)과 Maria Palmontier의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1749년 4월 29일 프랑스 바스-히 로스오에서 Marguerite Grandadam(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1802년 9월 25일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 콘와고 차플 바실리카 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Francis Nicholas Marshall nasceu em 30 de agosto de 1728, em Rothau, Bas-Rhin, França filho de Nicolas Marchal e Maria Palmontier. Ele casou-se com Marguerite Grandadam em 29 de abril de 1749, em Rothau, Bas-Rhin, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 25 de setembro de 1802, em Adams, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Conewago Chapel Basilica Cemetery, Adams, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Francis Nicholas Marshall родился 30 августа 1728 г. в местности ротао, бас-рин, Франция. Его родителей звали Nicolas Marchal и Maria Palmontier. Он женился на женщине по имени Marguerite Grandadam 29 апреля 1749 г. в местности ротао, бас-рин, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер 25 сентября 1802 г. в местности адамс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности коунюаго чапел басилика кеметири, адамс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Francis Nicholas Marshall nació el 30 de agosto de 1728, en Rothau, Bas-Rhin, Francia como hijo de Nicolas Marchal y Maria Palmontier. Se casó con Marguerite Grandadam el 29 de abril de 1749, en Rothau, Bas-Rhin, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió el 25 de septiembre de 1802, en Adams, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Conewago Chapel Basilica Cemetery, Adams, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos.
